name: Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
directions: Boil the rice and keep hot and dry.
Cut off the top crust of the bread.
Hollow out the inside.
Fry the oysters.
Butter the inside of the loaf well and place in the oven to brown.
Fill with the hot fried oysters.
The top of the loaf of bread may be buttered, toasted and placed on the oysters to retain the heat, although the oysters stay more crispy if served uncovered.
Serve on a platter with hot boiled rice which is seasoned, dotted with butter, and garnished with minced parsley and paprika.
Note: A finely minced clove of garlic may be used to flavor the butter.

similar.name: Oysters on the Half Shell With Oriental Mignonette

name: Rolled Oysters
directions: Cook the oatmeal in the 5 cups of water.
Add the corn syrup to the oatmeal and remove from heat.
Set the oatmeal mixture aside to cool and then add the evaporated milk and the reserved liquid from oysters.
Add the baking powder and flour, beat thoroughly, and then add the egg yolks into the oatmeal mixture.
Beat the egg whites until stiff and then fold the egg whites into the oat mixture.
Add a little more self-rising flour if the oat batter is too watery (it should have the consistency of wet mud).
Roll each oyster in cracker crumbs, dip it into the oat batter and then roll in the cracker crumbs a second time.
(At this point, the oysters can be placed on wax paper and stored in the freezer until you are ready to fry them, or fried immediately.) To prepare, deep fry until they turn a dark brown color.
They may also be pan fried.

similar.name: Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
Oysters on the Half Shell With Oriental Mignonette

name: Oysters on the Half Shell With Oriental Mignonette
directions: Combine first 4 ingredients in small bowl.
Let stand 15 minutes.

similar.name: Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
Tarleton Twins' Broiled Oysters on Half Shell

name: Oyster Puffs
directions: Comine flour, baking powder, salt and pepper.
Stir in milk to make batter.
Add drained oysters and coat thoroughly.
Fill fry pan with oil to depth of 1-1 1/2 inches.
Remove oysters with fork and drop into hot oil.
Fry on bothg sides to golden brown.

similar.name: Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
Stove Top Smoker Oysters on the Half Shell

name: Barbecued Oysters
directions: Combine egg and milk in a small bowl; mix well.
Dredge each oyster in flour, dip in egg mixture, and coat with cracker crumbs.  Set aside.
Saute onion in butter in a large skillet until tender.
Add next 7 ingredients, and mix well.
Bring mixture to a boil. reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, 10 minutes, stirring frequently.
Heat 1 inch of oil to 350ºF.  Fry oysters in oil until golden brown; drain on paper towels.
Place oysters in sauce and simmer, uncovered 5 minutes, turning once.
Serve immediately.

similar.name: Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
Tarleton Twins' Broiled Oysters on Half Shell

name: Stove Top Smoker Oysters on the Half Shell
directions: Put the wood chips in the bottom of the smoker.
Replace the bottom tray, and set in the rack.
Lay the oysters in the half shell on the smoking rack.
Start the oysters over medium heat and lower the heat slightly once the smoker lid is closed.
Smoke until the oysters are very lightly colored and firm, but still plump and moist, 9 minutes over the heat after closing the smoker lid and about 6 minutes off the heat with the smoker lid closed.
Check the oysters for doneness after they have been off the heat 5 minutes.
Serve right from the smoker or drizzle them with melted butter and pass lemon wedges, a pepper mill, and a bottle of hot red pepper sauce.
Or prepare your favorite stuffed oyster recipe, fold into a spinach omelet, or try them in Oysters Vanderbilt (see related separate recipe).

similar.name: Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
Tarleton Twins' Broiled Oysters on Half Shell

name: Oysters With Chilli Dressing
directions: Cut shallots (spring onions) into 5 cm lengths then shred legthwise very finely.
Put the sweet chilli sauce, lime juice and olive oil in a bowl and mix until well combined. Add finely chopped coriander.

Arrange the oysters on a platter on a bed of crushed ice. Place a couple of shreds of shallot (spring onion) on each oyster and serve immediately, either with a little of the dressing spooned over each oyster or, if you prefer, with the dressing served separately.

similar.name: Oysters on the Half Shell With Oriental Mignonette
Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice
Tarleton Twins' Broiled Oysters on Half Shell

name: Tarleton Twins' Broiled Oysters on Half Shell
directions: Wash oyster shells and pry open, but do not pour off liquid.
Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Combine bacon, parsley, and onion; sprinkle over oysters, and top with breadcrumbs.
Place on broiling pan and broil under medium heat 2-5 minutes or until edges begin to curl.
Serve hot as an appetizer.

similar.name: Oysters on the Half Shell With Oriental Mignonette
Old Canadian Oysters With Bread and Rice

name: Fried Oysters
directions: Pour vegetable oil to a depth of 3" in a heavy Dutch oven and heat over medium-high heat to 350 degrees.
Combine next 5 ingredients in a shallow bowl. Dredge oysters in mixture to coat.
Fry oysters in batches until golden brown 3-4 minutes per batch. Drain on paper towels and serve immediately.
